data.title: Physiomics Board Urges Shareholders to Reject Investor-Led Board Overhaul
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Physiomics ( (GB:PYC) ) just unveiled an update.

Physiomics has published a circular convening a requisitioned general meeting on 29 April 2026 after shareholder Michael Whitlow, who holds about 12.35% of the company, sought to overhaul the board by appointing three new directors and removing four existing ones, including the CEO and chairman. The board, which had attempted but failed to reach a negotiated compromise offering two non-executive seats to the requisitioning group, unanimously urges shareholders to vote against all the resolutions, arguing the move is premature, potentially disruptive and lacks a credible alternative plan, thereby framing the meeting as a key test of control and governance direction at the AIM-listed firm.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:PYC) stock is a Sell with a £0.43 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Physiomics stock, see the GB:PYC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PYC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PYC is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses and persistent cash burn), partially offset by a low-leverage balance sheet and some improvement in price trend. Valuation is constrained by negative earnings and lack of dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on PYC stock, click here.

More about Physiomics

Physiomics plc is a UK-based company operating on London Stock Exchange’s AIM market, focused on applying advanced modelling and analytics to support drug development and related life sciences decision-making. The company targets pharmaceutical and biotech clients, positioning itself as a specialist provider of data-driven services to improve clinical and R&D outcomes.

Average Trading Volume: 7,982,061

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £2.06M

Learn more about PYC stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Sage cancels 1.55m shares in ongoing London buyback
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sage Group plc ( (GB:SGE) ) has provided an update.

The Sage Group plc has continued executing its previously announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 1,552,493 ordinary shares on 2 April 2026 via Morgan Stanley at a volume-weighted average price of 850.82p. The shares, bought within a price range of 838.6p to 865.2p on the London Stock Exchange and multilateral trading facilities, will all be cancelled, marginally reducing the company’s share count and enhancing capital returns to shareholders.

The latest tranche forms part of a buyback initiative scheduled to run until no later than early June 2026, underscoring Sage’s confidence in its balance sheet strength and long-term strategy. By shrinking its equity base through cancellations rather than holding the stock in treasury, the group is likely to support earnings per share and signal a continued commitment to disciplined capital allocation in a competitive software and services market.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:SGE) stock is a Sell with a £8.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sage Group plc stock, see the GB:SGE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SGE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SGE is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance and a constructive earnings outlook with margin expansion and durable recurring revenue growth. This is tempered by very weak technicals (downtrend and heavy negative momentum) and a valuation that remains relatively expensive on P/E despite a moderate dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SGE stock, click here.

More about Sage Group plc

The Sage Group plc is a UK-listed provider of business management software and services, best known for its accounting, payroll and enterprise resource planning solutions targeted at small and mid-sized enterprises. The company focuses on cloud-based platforms and subscription models, competing in the broader business software and fintech ecosystem across its core markets.

Average Trading Volume: 4,670,080

data.title: Balance Labs Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Balance Labs ( (BLNC) ) has released a notification of late filing.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Balance Labs, Inc. has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors that it will delay its Annual Report on Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the year ended December 31, 2025. The company, based in Miami Beach, says the delay affects its full-year reporting but does not involve any other overdue SEC filings.

Management explains that the compilation, distribution and review of the financial information for the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) have put heavy time pressure on both the company and its auditor. These workload and timing constraints mean filing on the original deadline would have required unreasonable effort and expense.

Balance Labs expects to submit the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) no later than the fifteenth calendar day after the original due date. This timing is consistent with the grace period allowed under SEC Rule 12b-25 for late annual filings.

The company does not anticipate any significant change in its results of operations compared to the prior fiscal year once the Form 10-K (Yearly Report) is filed. As with all such notices, investors should treat these expectations as forward-looking statements that could change once the final audit and review are completed.

Balance Labs affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, underscoring ongoing efforts to remain in compliance with SEC reporting rules. The notification is signed by Chief Executive Officer Alan Campbell, who serves as the principal executive officer and formally authorizes the late-filing notice on behalf of the company.

More about Balance Labs

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $72.62M

Find detailed analytics on BLNC stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Pender Growth Fund Lifts Stake in Software Arm as BroadCare Deal Closes
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Pender Growth Fund Inc. ( (TSE:PTF) ) is now available.

Pender Growth Fund Inc. has increased its equity stake in Pender Software Holdings Ltd. to approximately 94.6% following the completion of PSH’s acquisition of UK-based BroadCare Solutions Limited, a long-standing provider of patient management software to the National Health Service. The transaction involved a fresh cash injection and the conversion of a promissory note into PSH equity, bringing PTF’s total cost base in PSH to about $20 million and deepening its exposure to a portfolio that now includes a mission-critical healthcare platform with strong recurring revenue.

BroadCare’s suite of products, used by roughly 75% of Integrated Care Boards in the UK and covering the full continuing care patient pathway, will continue to be run by its existing management team, preserving operational continuity. For PSH, this marks its third acquisition and is expected to lift pro forma revenue from about $13 million to more than $20 million while maintaining EBITDA margins above 30%, reinforcing PTF’s strategy of compounding value through scalable, high-margin software assets and bolstering its positioning in the healthcare technology segment.

More about Pender Growth Fund Inc.

Pender Growth Fund Inc. is a Vancouver-based investment company focused on achieving long-term capital appreciation by investing in small cap, special situations, and illiquid public and private companies. The fund trades on the TSX Venture Exchange under the symbol PTF and leverages a small capital base and long-term horizon to back unique, often underfollowed opportunities, including a controlling interest in Pender Software Holdings Ltd., which acquires and grows software businesses.

YTD Price Performance: 0.71%

Average Trading Volume: 2,797

For an in-depth examination of PTF stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

data.title: Foxtons CFO Receives Net Share Award as Treasury Shares Released
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Foxtons ( (GB:FOXT) ) just unveiled an update.

Foxtons Group has disclosed a share-based remuneration event involving its chief financial officer, Christopher Hough, under its 2020 Restricted Share Plan. Following the vesting of awards granted in April 2022 and April 2023, Hough became entitled to 1,107,169 ordinary shares, with 520,369 of those withheld to meet tax obligations arising from the vesting.

The net 586,800 ordinary shares are being released from treasury and transferred to Hough, with no shares sold in the market, meaning there is no immediate impact on the company’s free float. The transaction, conducted outside a trading venue and notified under UK Market Abuse Regulation, highlights Foxtons’ ongoing use of equity-based incentives to align executive remuneration with shareholder interests while managing tax liabilities through net settlement.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:FOXT) stock is a Buy with a £49.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Foxtons stock, see the GB:FOXT Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FOXT is a Neutral.

The score is supported by improving fundamentals (profitability since 2022, deleveraging, positive cash generation) and attractive valuation (low P/E with a dividend). These are partially offset by weak technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD) and earnings-call risks around sales underperformance, margin/cost pressure, and near-term working-capital headwinds despite positive 2026 growth guidance.

data.title: Dishman Carbogen Amcis Wins Waivers on Key NCD Financial Covenants
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd. ( (IN:DCAL) ) has shared an announcement.

Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd. disclosed that its debenture trustee, Axis Trustee Services Ltd., has consented to amend certain financial covenants for the financial year ended March 31, 2026, tied to a Rs 50 crore tranche of non-convertible debentures issued under ISIN INE385W07059. While the net debt to tangible net worth covenant remains unchanged, the company obtained waivers for covenants relating to total net debt to EBITDA, adjusted debt service coverage ratio, and interest coverage ratio on a consolidated basis, providing added flexibility in meeting its debt obligations and potentially easing short-term financial pressure for both the issuer and debenture holders.

More about Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd.

Dishman Carbogen Amcis Ltd. is a listed Indian company with both equity and debt securities traded on BSE and the National Stock Exchange. The company raises funds through privately placed, senior secured, rated, listed, taxable, redeemable non-convertible debentures, reflecting an active presence in the domestic debt capital markets.

Average Trading Volume: 38,575

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: 22.87B INR

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

The latest announcement is out from Greencore ( (GB:GNC) ).

Greencore has issued and allotted 593,391 ordinary shares of £0.01 each between 28 September 2025 and 1 April 2026 to satisfy employee options under its UK Share Save Scheme, with these shares already covered by a prior block admission and fully fungible with existing stock. In addition, the company secured a new block admission in March 2026 for up to 12,000,000 further ordinary shares to meet future share scheme obligations, taking its total shares in issue to 803,533,795 and underscoring ongoing use of equity-based employee incentives without altering its market listing status.

Greencore is a leading convenience food manufacturer serving UK and Irish retail and food service customers, with its shares traded on the London Stock Exchange Main Market. The group has expanded its equity capacity by admitting up to 12 million new shares while issuing nearly 600,000 shares to honour UK employee share save options, maintaining full fungibility with existing stock and signalling continued reliance on share-based compensation within its capital management strategy.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:GNC) stock is a Buy with a £298.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Greencore stock, see the GB:GNC Stock Forecast page.

Baronsmead Second Venture Trust PLC reported that non-executive directors Graham McDonald and Timothy Farazmand have acquired additional ordinary shares through the company’s Dividend Reinvestment Plan.
